description_long: This is a classic red fire hydrant with a domed top and a hexagonal base, commonly found in urban environments. The hydrant features two side hose connection outlets and a larger front outlet, each capped with hexagonal nuts. The surface is painted in a weathered red, showing signs of wear and some minor scuffing, which adds to its realistic appearance. The top of the hydrant has a central operating nut, and the body is reinforced with vertical ridges for structural strength. There are no visible chains attached to the caps, and the hydrant appears to be made primarily of cast metal. Subtle details such as bolts around the base and faint markings on the front add to its authenticity.
description: A weathered red fire hydrant with three capped outlets, a domed top, and a hexagonal base, featuring realistic wear and structural details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main outlet with a large hexagonal cap and faint markings on the body.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting one side outlet and the domed top, with visible bolts on the base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the smooth back surface and both side outlets, with no visible markings.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the other side outlet and the main front outlet cap, with clear view of the base bolts.
